markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose backend APIs for symbol lookup, definition, references, callers, tests, owning WP, and source spans. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeNavigationApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeNavigationApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: STALENESS-SILENTLY-SERVED (spec 2.3.13.11 'mark stale, never serve stale silently'): the staleness module docstring (staleness.rs:10) and the MT contract claim the nav API 'attaches the resulting StalenessVerdict to every served symbol'. FALSE. Only file_lens (1 of 6 routes) returns staleness (via build_monaco_payload). lookup_symbols, get_symbol, symbol_references, symbol_tests, symbol_spans return symbols with NO staleness field at all (symbol_to_json, knowledge_code_nav.rs:257-298, emits nothing). A symbol whose source file changed is served as authoritative through 5 of 6 routes with no freshness flag - the exact 'serve stale silently' failure the spec forbids. The nav-API test only asserts staleness on the lens route (test line 354), so this gap is untested and the contract claim is unmet.

Scalability/DoS: lookup_symbols (knowledge_code_nav.rs:313) and the lens (monaco_bridge.rs:128) call list_knowledge_entities_by_kind which SELECTs ALL symbol entities in the workspace, then filter in Rust by name/path/prefix. At a real repo (100k+ symbols) every lookup transfers and heap-allocates the entire symbol set; combined with the per-symbol N+1 span/edge queries in symbol_to_json/monaco it is O(workspace_symbols) per request. The 'precise backend navigation' claim does not hold at scale - there is no name/path index pushdown to SQL.

decode_path (knowledge_code_nav.rs:638) re-replaces %2F->'/' AFTER axum already percent-decoded the segment; a caller who double-encodes ('%252F') yields a literal '%2F' that won't match an indexed file (lookup miss, not an injection) - benign but surprising. No '..' rejection on the :path segment either; today it only feeds get_knowledge_entity_by_identity (a bind), so not exploitable, but unguarded.
evasion_probes: VERIFIED all 6 routes call nav_context(&headers)? as the FIRST fallible step (knowledge_code_nav.rs:307,364,381,443,492,543) -> 400 if x-hsk-actor-id / kernel-task-run-id / session-run-id missing. The integration test only probes the lookup route for 400, but source inspection confirms all 6 enforce it. Unknown actor_kind -> 400 (knowledge_code_nav.rs:181).

VERIFIED every route appends record_nav_receipt(...).await? carrying actor+session+correlation verbatim (KnowledgeRetrievalTraceRecorded) BEFORE returning the body, and threads nav_receipt_event_id into the response. A receipt build/append failure -> 500, no data served (fail-closed). No route returns data without a receipt.

SQL-INJECTION: NEGATIVE. The name/path query params are NEVER concatenated into SQL; filtering is in-Rust over a bound list query. The :path and :entity_id segments are passed to parameterised queries ($1) via get_knowledge_entity_by_identity / get_knowledge_entity. symbol-name params cannot inject.
PATH-INJECTION: NEGATIVE for DB (binds); relative_path only reaches get_knowledge_entity_by_identity as a bound value, not a filesystem path, in the nav layer.
Confirmed require_symbol 404s missing ids and 400s non-symbol entity kinds (knowledge_code_nav.rs:580) - type confusion (passing a file/concept entity id) is rejected.
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_code_nav_api_tests.rs (happy path + 400 + 404; staleness asserted only on lens)
runtime: code-index lib 43 tests green; nav-API integration test present (PG-gated)

hardening_pending.reason: HIGH: staleness SILENTLY SERVED - contract/docstring claim StalenessVerdict attached to every served symbol but nav API does not (spec 2.3.13.11 mark-stale-never-serve-stale-silently violation); load-all-workspace-symbols then Rust-filter DoS.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
